notes: Iteration-2 assertions target reasoning quality, not just correct output. Each eval asserts a mix of: (a) named-mechanism citation, (b) reference/section pointer, (c) version/compiler caveat when relevant, (d) measure-vs-hypothesis framing for snippet-only work.

evals.name: optimize-word-counter
evals.expected_output: Optimized Count function with book-level rationale.

assertions.text: Names the map-lookup string-conversion elision (rule that `m[string(b)]` read does not allocate but write/compound does).
assertions.check_hint: rationale contains 'elision' or explicit phrasing like 'read does not allocate but write does'

assertions.text: Names the double-hash penalty of `m[k] = m[k] + 1` vs single-hash `m[k]++`.
assertions.check_hint: rationale mentions hash twice/double hash/one hash, OR cites §11.2

assertions.text: Names buffer reuse (`word = word[:0]`) as the reason the scratch slice stops allocating per word.
assertions.check_hint: mentions buffer reuse, amortized, backing array retained, or zero-alloc after first word

assertions.text: Cites at least one specific section or rule from the skill references (e.g. §9.1.3, §11.2, `strings.md`, `maps.md`).
assertions.check_hint: matches §\d+\.\d+ or a references/*.md filename

assertions.text: Either acknowledges Go 1.19 baseline for the elision rule OR notes the pointer-value counter trick (`map[string]*int`) with its trade-off.
assertions.check_hint: mentions 1.19, Go toolchain version, OR map[string]*int trade-off for mutation-heavy

assertions.text: Explicitly justifies byte-level whitespace detection as UTF-8-safe (continuation bytes always ≥ 0x80).
assertions.check_hint: mentions 0x80, continuation byte, ASCII whitespace safety, or UTF-8 boundary preserved

assertions.text: Public signature `Count(input []byte) map[string]int` preserved.
assertions.check_hint: signature match

assertions.text: Code is syntactically valid Go (parses).
assertions.check_hint: package declaration + no obvious syntax errors

evals.name: escape-review
evals.expected_output: Structured analysis (observed/hypothesis/verify), escape-by-escape breakdown, rewrite, clearly labeled hypotheses.

assertions.text: Enumerates ≥3 distinct escape sources in the original: (a) &t loop-variable escape, (b) fmt routing through reflect.ValueOf, (c) non-preallocated append.
assertions.check_hint: all three enumerated as separate items with mechanism names

assertions.text: Names `reflect.ValueOf` (or fmt's use of it) as the mechanism that forces escape, per §4.6.3.
assertions.check_hint: mentions reflect.ValueOf explicitly, ideally citing §4.6.3

assertions.text: Names the loop-variable address-escape rule per §4.6 (`&t` inside `for _, t := range`).
assertions.check_hint: mentions &t escape or §4.6 or 'address of loop variable'

assertions.text: Cites at least two specific sections (§4.6, §4.6.3, §14.1, etc.) from the skill references.
assertions.check_hint: matches ≥2 §\d+\.\d+

assertions.text: Labels claims as hypothesis/expected/to-verify rather than asserting measured speedups without measurement (snippet-only branch of SKILL.md).
assertions.check_hint: mentions 'expected', 'hypothesis', 'run scripts/…', 'verify with benchstat', or equivalent

assertions.text: Acknowledges the irreducible ≥1-alloc floor (the returned []string elements) OR proposes a signature change to remove it.
assertions.check_hint: mentions ≥1 alloc floor, returned-string must heap-allocate, OR API redesign

assertions.text: Rewrite preallocates `out` with `make([]string, 0, len(tickets))` (or equivalent known-length form).
assertions.check_hint: make([]string, 0, len(tickets)) or make([]string, len(tickets))

assertions.text: Rewrite inner loop contains no `fmt.Sprintf`/`fmt.Println` (uses strconv.Append* or a local []byte scratch).
assertions.check_hint: no fmt.* inside the for-body of the rewrite block

assertions.text: Rewrite uses `&tickets[i]` or by-value pattern (not `&t` of loop variable).
assertions.check_hint: rewrite has for i := range + &tickets[i], OR drops pointer entirely

evals.name: merge-slices-hot
evals.expected_output: Two-pass preallocation (either shape), honest benchmark, named rule.

assertions.text: Names the two-pass preallocation pattern (`memory-allocations.md` Rule 1 / §8.6 grow-in-one-step).
assertions.check_hint: mentions two-pass, single allocation, pre-size, or cites §8.6 / §3.6

assertions.text: Cites at least one specific section from `memory-allocations.md` or `slices.md`.
assertions.check_hint: matches §3.6, §8.6, §8.9, or the file name

assertions.text: Either implements or notes the integer-overflow guard on the length sum (book's production-grade form of two-pass).
assertions.check_hint: mentions overflow, `if k := n + len(s); k < n` pattern, or a caveat

assertions.text: Notes the make+copy zero-fill elision detail (Go 1.15+, §8.3 restrictions) OR explains why preferring make+append over make+copy is a defensible choice for readability.
assertions.check_hint: mentions zero-fill, memclr elision, 1.15, §8.3, or acknowledges trade-off

assertions.text: Benchmark does setup before the measured region and calls `b.ResetTimer()` after it.
assertions.check_hint: ResetTimer after input build

assertions.text: Benchmark uses `b.ReportAllocs()`.
assertions.check_hint: contains b.ReportAllocs()

assertions.text: Benchmark exercises both old and new implementations (sub-benchmarks or twin funcs) for apples-to-apples comparison.
assertions.check_hint: b.Run with Old/New OR two Benchmark funcs

assertions.text: Output of optimized function is assigned somewhere that defeats dead-code elimination (global sink, `runtime.KeepAlive`, etc.).
assertions.check_hint: package-level sink variable, runtime.KeepAlive, or result used after loop

assertions.text: Includes a correctness/equivalence test (not just a benchmark) so the refactor is guarded.
assertions.check_hint: TestConcat* or reflect.DeepEqual / bytes.Equal check

evals.name: interface-boxing
evals.expected_output: Named-mechanism analysis + concrete fast path + the canonical stdlib answer reference.

assertions.text: Gives a specific per-pixel allocation count (≥2) and explains where each comes from (At → Color interface, Set → color.Color boxing).
assertions.check_hint: states a number of allocs/pixel AND identifies both At and Set as sources

assertions.text: Names interface boxing (§14.1) and/or virtual-method-call + no-inline penalty (§14.3).
assertions.check_hint: mentions §14.1 / §14.3 / 'boxing' / 'vtable' / 'virtual call'

assertions.text: Mentions `image/draw.RGBA64Image` / `RGBA64At` as the stdlib's documented answer (§14.4 canonical example).
assertions.check_hint: mentions RGBA64Image or RGBA64At

assertions.text: Cites at least one section from `interfaces.md` and ideally one from `bounds-check-elim.md`.
assertions.check_hint: matches §14.x + optionally §10.x

assertions.text: Rewrite uses `PixOffset` (once per row, not per pixel) or direct `Pix[]` indexing.
assertions.check_hint: PixOffset outside inner loop + Pix[] indexing inside

assertions.text: Applies at least one BCE technique (3-index slice, highest-index-first, row-local rebind). Names it.
assertions.check_hint: mentions 3-index / [:n:n] / 'highest-index-first' / §10.6 / 'BCE'

assertions.text: No fallback to generic `image.Image` — fast path is concrete to `*image.RGBA` per task constraint.
assertions.check_hint: function signature is *image.RGBA, not image.Image

assertions.text: Per-pixel arithmetic stays in integer space and is justified (e.g., `v >> 1` is exact for 8-bit darkening).
assertions.check_hint: r >> 1 or v/2 on uint8 with correctness comment
